# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { openKV } from "deno/kv";
import { parse as urlParse } from "https://deno.land/std@0.167.0/encoding/url.ts";

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
}

async function searchHandler(req: Request) {
  const kv = await openKV();
  const query = new URL(req.url).searchParams;

  if (!query.has("filter")) {
    return new Response(`Error: Missing 'filter' parameter`, { status: 400 });
  }

  const filterParam = urlParse(query.get("filter")!).toString();

  try {
    const userIds: string[] = [];
    const result = await kv.iterate(['users', "*"]);
    for await (const [key, value] of result) {
      const userKeyParts = key.split("/");
      if (userKeyParts.length === 3 && userKeyParts[1] !== filterParam) continue;
      
      userIds.push(userKeyParts[0]);
    }

    const users = [];
    for (const id of userIds) {
      const userData: User = await kv.get(['users', id, "name"]);
      const userObject: { [key: string]: User } = {};
      if (!userData) continue;

      const userEmail: string | null = await kv.get(['users', id, 'email']);
      userObject[name] = userData;
      userObject[email] = userEmail ?? "";

      users.push(userObject);
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(users));
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    return new Response(`Error: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
  }
}

async function main() {
  const handler = async (req: RequestEvent) => {
    try {
      await searchHandler(req);
    } catch (error) {
      return new Response(`Internal Server Error: ${error.message}`, { status: 500 });
    }
  };

  return handlers({
    GET: handler,
  });
}

main();
